reasoning: Initial observation shows d1=10, d2=20, d3=30 and total_distance=60. The sum of the segments (10+20+30) equals the total distance. To confirm this relationship holds when variables change, I will increase d1 and observe if total_distance increases by the same amount.

Previous step increased d1 from 10 to 20, and total_distance increased from 60 to 70. The difference is exactly 10, matching the change in d1. This suggests total_distance = d1 + d2 + d3. To further verify, I will change d2 and observe the effect on total_distance.

Previous steps showed that increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, and increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. This linear relationship suggests total_distance is the sum of the segments. The current state (d1=20, d2=30, d3=30) yields a total_distance of 80, which equals 20+30+30. To be thorough, I will test changing d3 to confirm the pattern holds for the third variable.

The observations consistently show that increasing any single segment (d1, d2, or d3) results in an equal increase in total_distance. Specifically, changing d1 by +10 changed total_distance by +10, changing d2 by +10 changed total_distance by +10, and changing d3 by +10 changed total_distance by +10. The current state (d1=20, d2=30, d3=40) yields total_distance=90, which is exactly 20+30+40. This confirms the relationship is a simple sum.
